Caption: All-solid-state composite cathode half-cell comprised of NMC active particles, nanocarbon, and Li 6 PS 5 Cl solid electrolyte.
(a) sharp and (b) diffuse crack representations, and (c) crack distribution profiles.

Heterogeneous composite cathode structure with ≈ 15 % porosity is developed based on a scanning-electron-microscopy (SEM) image reported by Doerrer et al. (2021). The solid electrolyte is colored as yellow, the active material is grey, and voids are white.
Electro-chemo-mechanical initial and boundary conditions. The transport boundary conditions are highlighted in red, and the mechanics boundary conditions are marked in gray.
Critical energy release rate distribution with G c = 2 . 79 J m−2 for LPSC, G c = 2 . 50 J m−2 for NMC, and G c = 1 . 00 J m−2 for the electrode-electrolyte interfaces.
Finite element mesh distribution, showing significantly finer meshes at interfaces to maintain the phase-field fracture accuracy.
Voltage profiles as a function of capacity under 0.02 C, 0.1 C and 5 C charge rates.
(a) Voltage-capacity profiles at the end of 0.1 C charging rates with (b) Li concentration and (c) first-principal stress distribution at the end of charge.
